qa_pairs.question: How can volumes be automatically mounted when a virtual machine boots?
qa_pairs.answer: Volumes can be automatically mounted when a virtual machine boots by editing the file named `/etc/fstab` to contain a new line with details about how the volume should be mounted.

qa_pairs.question: Which file needs to be edited to set up automatic volume mounting at VM boot?
qa_pairs.answer: The file named `/etc/fstab` needs to be edited to configure automatic volume mounting when a virtual machine boots.

qa_pairs.question: What command should be used to view mounting information?
qa_pairs.answer: To view mounting information, use the `blkid` command.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of identifier is used when adding a line to `/etc/fstab` for automatic mounting?
qa_pairs.answer: A UUID (Universally Unique Identifier) is used when adding a line to `/etc/fstab` for automatic mounting.

qa_pairs.question: Provide an example of a line to add to `/etc/fstab` for automatically mounting a volume.
qa_pairs.answer: An example line to add to `/etc/fstab` is: `/dev/disk/by-uuid/anananan-anan-anana-anan-ananananana /mnt auto defaults,nofail 0 3`.
